subject: [PATCH] NFSD: restart ssc_expire_umount walk after dropping nfsd_ssc_lock

reviews.author: Jeff Layton
reviews.summary: Identified a potential stale pointer issue and suggested restarting the walk after dropping the lock. Approved the patch with minor comment on the comment clarity.
reviews.raw_body:
On Sat, 2026-05-23 at 07:02 -0400, Michael Bommarito wrote:
> On Sat, May 23, 2026 at 6:55 AM Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org> wrote:
> > Comment is a bit confusing, given that you replaced
> > list_for_each_entry_safe() with list_for_each_entry().
> 
> Sorry, that's left over from an earlier patch attempt that introduced
> a different issue.  How would this comment look?
> 
> Concurrent nfsd4_ssc_cancel_dul() can free an item while spinlock is
> dropped for mntput() above, so restart the walk from the head so no
> stale pointer is followed.


Sure, looks good. FWIW, the "restart the loop after dropping the lock"
pattern is pretty common across the kernel, but it is good to lay out
the rationale in a comment.
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Michael Bommarito
reviews.summary: Clarified the comment and explained the rationale behind restarting the walk after dropping the lock.
reviews.raw_body:
On Sat, May 23, 2026 at 6:55 AM Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org> wrote:
> Comment is a bit confusing, given that you replaced
> list_for_each_entry_safe() with list_for_each_entry().

Sorry, that's left over from an earlier patch attempt that introduced
a different issue.  How would this comment look?

Concurrent nfsd4_ssc_cancel_dul() can free an item while spinlock is
dropped for mntput() above, so restart the walk from the head so no
stale pointer is followed.

Thanks,
Mike

reviews.author: Jeff Layton
reviews.summary: Approved the revised patch and noted that the 'restart the loop after dropping the lock' pattern is common across the kernel.
reviews.raw_body:
On Sat, 2026-05-23 at 07:02 -0400, Michael Bommarito wrote:
> On Sat, May 23, 2026 at 6:55 AM Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org> wrote:
> > Comment is a bit confusing, given that you replaced
> > list_for_each_entry_safe() with list_for_each_entry().
> 
> Sorry, that's left over from an earlier patch attempt that introduced
> a different issue.  How would this comment look?
> 
> Concurrent nfsd4_ssc_cancel_dul() can free an item while spinlock is
> dropped for mntput() above, so restart the walk from the head so no
> stale pointer is followed.


Sure, looks good. FWIW, the "restart the loop after dropping the lock"
pattern is pretty common across the kernel, but it is good to lay out
the rationale in a comment.
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-05-23.patch_summary: Patch addresses a potential stale pointer issue in NFSD by restarting the ssc_expire_umount walk after dropping nfsd_ssc_lock.
